scenario: Pinnacle Marketplace runs Camunda 8.8 on the order-process model with 5000 orders per day for the retailer. The on-call operator must recall which variable the XOR gateway evaluation reads on every newly created process instance so the marketplace's routing path through the order-process diagram stays correct on the broker.
question: Which variable does the order-process model require on every new process instance for the XOR gateway evaluation to happen properly?

options.text: The orderId variable is required on every new process instance so the XOR gateway evaluation can compare the order identifier against the 100 threshold defined on the outgoing sequence flow
The customerId variable is required on every new process instance so the XOR gateway evaluation can route the order through the higher-value or lower-value path shown for the retailer in Operate
The orderValue variable is required on every new process instance so the XOR gateway evaluation can route the instance along the correct outgoing sequence flow inside the order-process model
The paymentStatus variable is required on every new process instance so the XOR gateway evaluation can split traffic between the insured shipping and uninsured shipping paths in the broker

a.text: Incorrect. The documentation names orderValue, not orderId, as the variable the XOR gateway evaluates; orderId is used in the example payload to identify the order but does not drive the gateway condition on the model.

b.text: Incorrect. customerId does not appear in the documented order-process example; the XOR gateway condition reads orderValue to decide between the higher-value and lower-value routing paths in the model.

c.text: Correct. The documentation states that every process instance created for the order-process model requires an orderValue so the XOR gateway evaluation will happen properly and route the instance along the right path.

d.text: Incorrect. paymentStatus is not the variable the order-process XOR gateway reads; the documented condition uses orderValue to choose between the outgoing sequence flows after Initiate Payment.

explanation: The Operate guide for resolving incidents states that every process instance created for the order-process model requires an orderValue variable so the XOR gateway evaluation can route the instance along the correct outgoing sequence flow.
